rows.Event Name: Buds-A-Palooza 2026
rows.Event Description: Downtown Phoenix’s 21+ Music, Arts & Counter Culture Festival—2 live music stages, murals, food & good vibes all night in Roosevelt Row! 🕓 📍 Buds-A-Palooza is back — bigger, louder, and wilder than ever. On April 11th, six city blocks of Roosevelt Row will come alive with live music, art, food, and good vibes as Phoenix’s favorite counter culture festival takes over the streets. We’re talking two stages of nonstop music, live glass blowing demos, massive murals being painted in real time, and 20+ food and dessert vendors bringing every kind of munchie imaginable. Add in six bars, local artisans, and plenty of surprises, and you’ve got one unforgettable night in the heart of downtown. From fiery glassblowing and massive murals to an interactive Buds-A-Palooza photo wall, this event is built for good times and even better memories. Whether you’re into reggae or hip-hop rhythms, street art, or just love being surrounded by fun people and good energy, you’ll fit right in at Buds-A-Palooza.

rows.Event Name: Filipino Food Festival
rows.Event Description: April is Filipino Food Month, and we’re celebrating the best way we know how… with a community food festival! Get ready to celebrate April - Filipino Food Month with music, food, and fun at the Filipinos Food Festival! Hosted by AZ Filipinos. This celebration will be a vibrant mix of tradition, talent, and togetherness. BYO Chair/Banig/Picnic Blanket :) Our mission is to connect Filipino Americans in Arizona through community building, culture, and celebration. AZ Filipinos aims to cultivate a vibrant, intergenerational Filipino community in Phoenix that honors our heritage, empowers our future, and creates inclusive spaces where every Filipino whether born here or newly arrived feels seen, celebrated, and connected.

rows.Event Name: Arizona Tiki Oasis 2026 - Seminar Tickets
rows.Event Description: Daytime Seminars are a big part of Arizona Tiki Oasis. You may purchase all seminar tickets a la carte. Tiki Oasis was founded upon a concern for architectural preservation of mid-century design. We are proud to partner with one of the most successful and exquisite examples of mid-century restoration and preservation in America — the Hotel Valley Ho! The Phoenix area, AKA the Valley of the Sun, has a long, rich history of Tiki Style. The warm weather of Scottsdale and the sumptuous comfort of the 4-Diamond resort make the Arizona Tiki Oasis weekend a relaxed and luxurious experience. Enjoy a wide range of resort activities while sipping tropical cocktails and wearing your festive aloha wear! Bask in a variety of curated experiences: sip crafted cocktails by top mixologists, browse the pop-up Art Show, relax in a cabana by the pool, schedule a soothing spa treatment, attend an educational seminar, relish the rooftop sunsets, sway to the Arizona Tiki Oasis soundtrack, reserve Hotel Valley Ho Supperclub Ticket, shop the outdoor marketplace with a variety of artists, makers and traders and, of course, enjoy the overall Tiki island-in-the-desert vibe.

rows.Event Name: Arizona Tiki Oasis
rows.Event Description: Welcome to Arizona Tiki Oasis, a mid-century, cocktail, island lifestyle meet-up, held in the middle of the desert! Enjoy a wide range of resort activities while sipping tropical cocktails and wearing your aloha wear. Bask in a variety of curated experiences: sip craft cocktails by top mixologists, browse the Pop-up Art Show, relax in a private cabana by the pool, schedule a soothing spa treatment, attend an educational seminar, relish the rooftop sunsets, sway to the Tiki Oasis soundtrack, reserve Hotel Valley Ho dinner tickets, shop the outdoor marketplace with a variety of artists, makers and traders and, of course, enjoy the Tiki island-in-the-desert vibe. Although many events are all ages such as the marketplace and seminars, the overall event is adult-oriented. Arizona Tiki Oasis benefits Arizona Preservation Foundation.

rows.Event Name: The Future of Design and Construction 2026 - U.S. Summit
rows.Event Description: Building Transformations is returning to Phoenix to present an Immersive Innovation Showcase Experience! As emerging technologies like robotics, AI, VR, 3D printing, and automation continue to advance, integrating them effectively into real-world design and construction workflows—without disrupting operations—has become increasingly complex. At this summit, participants will move through the Innovation Center experiencing technology in action. Demonstrations will showcase real-world applications and workflows, featuring field technology, digital tools, robotics, AI, and automation that are transforming how infrastructure is planned, built, and managed across North America. This is your opportunity to move beyond theory: get hands-on with the technologies, challenge outdated processes, gain practical insight from industry leaders, and return with actionable strategies to boost efficiency, reduce rework, and improve project outcomes. ‍

rows.Event Name: 2026 Digital Marketing Strategies Conference
rows.Event Description: The Digital Marketing Strategies Conference is a high-level executive planning event, designed to create high-performing automotive dealers. The Digital Marketing Strategies Conference is a high-level executive planning event, designed to assist automotive dealers with creating a market-dominating digital marketing strategy for the year ahead. Held annually, this event brings the top thought leaders from the automotive marketing industry to beautiful Scottsdale for an intimate conference experience unlike any other.

rows.Event Name: Remembering The Future With Herb Greene - Salon Screening at Taliesin West
rows.Event Description: Join us at Taliesin West's singular Pavilion theater for a salon-style , intimate screening on Greene and the Organic Architecture movement. Butler Housing Company Coldwell Banker Historic Homes Group Architect and filmmaker Lila Cohen journeys into the imaginative and complicated world of her 94-year-old great-uncle, Herb Greene, a visionary architect whose work emerged from the avant-garde teachings of Bruce Goff in 1950s Oklahoma, pioneering a movement that flourished, faded, and quietly took root far beyond its origins. The film offers an intimate portrait of a lifelong search to express human feeling through art and architecture, inviting contemporary audiences to rediscover the meaning and relevance of Organic Architecture. Taliesin West is a studio and home developed by the American architect Frank Lloyd Wright in Scottsdale, Arizona, United States. Named after Wright's Taliesin studio in Spring Green, Wisconsin, Taliesin West was Wright's winter home and studio from 1937 until his death in 1959. The Arizona Architectural Film Showcase is an annual celebration of architecture through cinema, conversation, and community. Presented in architecturally significant venues, the 2026 edition features three documentaries by independent filmmakers curated by Steve Weiss, Executive Director of Founded in 2002,

rows.Event Name: 28th Annual Children's Neuroscience Symposium
rows.Event Description: Stay up-to-date in assessment and management of complex neurological conditions, according to the most recent evidence-based medicine. The The Symposium provides an evidence-based approach in the latest advances in what participants need to know in the assessment and management of complex neurological conditions, offering participants an opportunity to hear from experts on how they manage complex neurological patients. Discussed throughout the conference is the importance of the primary care provider's role alongside the specialist in providing the most comprehensive continuum of care. Hear from are some of the most respected experts in the field who will provide these crucial updates in the treatment of the complex neuro-patient and how these advances directly relate to the care received from their primary care provider. Our goal is to improve the knowledge of primary care clinicians, so they are better equipped to provide accurate assessments and management of care, facilitate their interactions with the specialist and improve overall care to this group of patients. This course is designed for the primary care provider, advanced practice provider, nursing staff, clinical research scientists and those wanting a better understanding of the presented clinical information. Phoenix Children's Main Campus Phoenix Children’s Conference Center 2632 North 20th Street Phoenix, Arizona The cost of registration includes: tuition, CME certificate (In-person price includes: breakfast, lunch, refreshments during the meeting and Friday's reception). Net proceeds from this Symposium will go to support Barrow at Phoenix Children’s Neuroscience General Program Fund #1043. The full fee is payable upon registration. The Annual Children’s Neuroscience Symposium is an accredited program designed to enhance the practitioner’s knowledge of common and complex pediatric neurological disorders, including presenting signs and symptoms, recommendations for specialist consultation and new treatment advances. This activity has been planned and implemented in accordance with the accreditation requirements and policies of the Accreditation Council for Continuing Medical Education (ACCME) through the joint providership of The University of Arizona College of Medicine - Tucson and Pheonix Children’s. The University of Arizona College of Medicine - Tucson is accredited by the ACCME to provide continuing medical education for physicians. The University of Arizona College of Medicine – Tucson designates this live activity for a maximum of University of Arizona College of Medicine - Tucson Office of Continuing Medical Education adheres to the ACCME's Standards for Integrity and Independence in Accredited Continuing Education. Any individuals in a position to control the content of a CME activity, including faculty, planners, reviewers or others are required to disclose all financial relationships with ineligible companies (commercial interests). The CME office reviewers have nothing to disclose. All relevant financial relationships have been mitigated prior to the commencement of the activity. Dr. Hauptman has disclosed: Consulting Fee-Medtronic | Consulting Fee-GE Healthcare | Consulting Fee-Iota. Dr. Rastogi has disclosed: Consulting Fee-Theranica Bio-electronics, Ltd (Relationship has ended). All other individuals in a position to control the content of this CME activity have disclosed that they have no relevant financial relationships with ineligible companies that would constitute a conflict of interest concerning this CME activity.

rows.Event Name: Lihn Law Group 2026 Elder Care and Legal Symposium
rows.Event Description: Join us at the Lihn Law Group 2026 Elder Care and Legal Symposium for a day of learning innovative solutions on critical elder care issues. Professionals who serve older adults are often the first to recognize when something has begun to change. A client who once managed everything independently begins to struggle with decisions. A family senses vulnerability but isn’t sure what steps to take. Financial behavior shifts unexpectedly. Situations emerge gradually, without clear answers, and professionals are asked to help navigate what comes next. These moments carry real consequences — for safety, autonomy, and quality of life. The Lihn Law Group Elder Care & Legal Symposium was created to support professionals in exactly these moments. This interdisciplinary symposium brings together clinical, legal, advocacy, and investigative perspectives to help professionals strengthen awareness, deepen judgment, and respond thoughtfully when supporting older adults and vulnerable individuals. This year’s program will address critical issues professionals increasingly encounter in practice, including: • Recognizing and assessing diminished decision-making capacity Attendance is limited to preserve a meaningful and interactive professional environment. Early registration is encouraged. This symposium is designed for professionals who work with or support older adults, including: • Social workers If your work involves supporting older adults or vulnerable individuals, this symposium will provide practical insight and interdisciplinary perspective relevant to your role. • Eight (8) credits were approved, by NASW, for Licensed Social Workers. *Continuing education credits are currently pending approval for Case Managers. Professionals supporting older adults are often called upon to help navigate situations where there is uncertainty, vulnerability, and no clear roadmap. This symposium provides an opportunity to learn from interdisciplinary experts, strengthen professional judgment, and connect with others who share a commitment to protecting and supporting vulnerable individuals. This annual gathering reflects a shared responsibility among professionals to serve older adults with clarity, skill, and compassion.

rows.Event Name: 2026 Annual Arizona Genetics Alliance Education Conference
rows.Event Description: Join us at the 2026 Annual Arizona Genetics Alliance Education Conference for a day of learning and networking! The AGA is hosting our 6th annual education conference, a premier event dedicated to advancing knowledge and fostering collaboration within the genetic counseling community. The conference offers a unique platform for networking, learning, and sharing the latest developments in genetics and genetic counseling. Don’t miss this exciting opportunity to enhance your expertise and connect with colleagues! We are excited to offer a hybrid event this year. Attendees may join in-person or virtually. The in-person event will be held at the Check-in will start at 7 AM. See the agenda to learn more about the layout for the day. The various talks and activities will be held in room 173A/B. The National Society of Genetic Counselors (NSGC) has authorized Arizona Genetics Alliance to offer up to Financial assistance is also available for any member in need. Please email the AGA Education Committee at educationcommitteeaga@gmail.com to inquire about this.

rows.Event Name: Wine tasting to support people living with Multiple Sclerosis
rows.Event Description: Sip some wine, support MS warriors, and have a blast at our second annual wine tasting event! Join us for a fun and relaxed wine tasting event! Sip on delicious wines while showing your support for those living with Multiple Sclerosis. It's a great way to enjoy good company, great flavors, and make a difference. Don't miss out on this special gathering—cheers to raising awareness and having a fantastic time! Grab your tickets today and snag the best price! Each one lets you enjoy five wine tastings and a full pour, with 20–30 different wines to sample. When you find your favorite, feel free to buy a bottle to take home!

rows.Event Name: Healing The Root Women's Gathering

Whether you’re looking to make new friends or just enjoy a day filled with positive energy, this event is all about pure, authentic moments with the Father and each other that you will cherish forever. Don’t miss out on the fun but pivotal moment—let’s create some beautiful memories together!
